reasoning: Both reports confirm the critical build/install files (PKGBUILD, .install, wrapper script) are safe. The 'unsafe' verdict from qwen was based on application runtime behavior (Copilot CLI shim), which is outside AUR audit scope as it doesn't execute during package build/install.
disagreement_analysis: qwen incorrectly flagged application runtime code as a package threat, while deepseek's inconclusive verdict stemmed from technical failures to audit large files irrelevant to package safety.
coverage_issues: Both models wasted resources auditing application files (Copilot/Git extensions) that don't execute during makepkg
Models failed to prioritize only build-relevant files like PKGBUILD hooks
false_positives: qwen's 'unsafe' rating for copilotCLIShim.js: Normal VS Code extension behavior (remote script loading) mistaken for supply-chain risk
learnings: Improve file prioritization: Exclude application runtime files (extensions/, resources/) unless referenced in PKGBUILD
Implement size-based file skipping: Auto-ignore files >1MB unless critical to build
Enhance prompt clarity: Emphasize that only build/install phase execution is in-scope
Add context compression: Use summarization for large files when unavoidable
